segments.text: When we see milk, we see that it is white.
When we taste it, it appears that the milk is very palatable.
When we touch milk, it appears very cold.
When we smell milk, it appears to be very good flavor.
And when we hear, we understand it is called milk.
Perceiving milk with different senses, we say that it is something white,
something very delicious, something very aromatic, and so on.
Similarly, those who are trying to find the supreme godhead by mental speculation
And those who are trying to find the supreme godhead by yoga practice
may find him as localized super soul.
But those who are directly trying to approach the supreme truth
can see him face to face as the supreme person.
Ultimately, the supreme person is the destination of all different processes.
The fortunate person who, by following the principles of scriptures,
becomes completely purified of all material contamination,
surrenders unto the supreme lord as everything.
Just as one can appreciate the real taste of milk with the tongue,
and not with eyes, nostrils, or ears,
one can similarly appreciate the supreme truth perfectly
only through one path, devotional service.
This is also confirmed in the Bhagavad Gita,
If one wants to understand the absolute truth in perfection,
he must take to devotional service.
Of course, no one can understand the truth in perfection,
that is not possible for the infinitesimal living entities,
but the highest point of understanding by the living entity
is reached by discharge of devotional service, not otherwise.
one may come to impersonal effulgence of the supreme personality of Godhead.

is very extensive because Brahman is ananta,
